ul.li: Wrong path: native memcpy on x86 (little-endian byte order)

div.p: Implementation Critical: SHA-256 is big-endian (FIPS 180-4). The shift-and-mask construction is the endianness bridge — it produces identical byte sequences on ALL platforms. memcpy of a native integer produces platform-dependent results.
memcpy(digest, &H[0], 4) x86 stores LSB at lowest address (little-endian native byte order)
digest[0] x86 writes LSB first = 0xBF ← WRONG
Memory layout (wrong): digest[0..3] = { 0xBF, 0x16, 0x78, 0xBA } → hex string: "bf1678ba..." ✗ Digest does NOT match NIST vectors
